methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Kyutai is a Growth-stage AI & Software company founded by Xavier Niel that has raised €300M to date. The broader AI & Software sector comprises 38 public and private peers, though the company operates in a sparse competitive tier. The most relevant reference points include five high-profile private peers—Palantir, OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks—alongside a single public comparable, Palantir (PLTR).

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. Despite 38 same-sector companies existing, the narrow filters required for comparable analysis (matching stage, valuation tier, and business model maturity) yielded insufficient datapoints: zero same-stage peers, only two adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier companies. A traditional multiples-based approach relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise and misrepresentation given Kyutai's specialized frontier-tech positioning within a sparse peer universe.

The sparsity of direct comparables—particularly the absence of same-stage, same-tier peers—indicates that valuation must rely on alternative approaches (DCF, venture capital method, or transaction precedent analysis) rather than multiples triangulation. Any valuation range derived from this company would require explicit modeling of long-term cash generation or capital requirements rather than reliance on market multiples. Key uncertainties center on competitive differentiation relative to the named peers and the company's path to sustainable unit economics.
